paper_title: Diatom Sampling and Analysis in Khor Al-Zubair Lagoon

methods.description: Procedures for collecting epipelic and epilithic diatom samples seasonally from Khor Al-Zubair lagoon, measuring environmental parameters, preserving samples, and preparing diatom frustules for microscopic examination and imaging.

steps.description: Samples were collected seasonally between January to November 2020 during low tide level.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect environmental and biological samples seasonally at specified locations and conditions.

steps.description: Some of environmental parameters were measured at field including air and water temperature, salinity and hydrogen ion concentration (Table 1).
outputs: Environmental parameter values (air temperature, water temperature, salinity, pH)

steps.description: Epipelic diatom was collected randomly by scrapping 0.5 cm from the top of sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect epipelic diatom samples from sediment surface.

steps.description: Epilithic diatoms on stones, standing wood, metals and ropes that were submerged in high tide were also collected by scrapping their surface using sharp tools and toothbrush, while the ropes were cut into small pieces then was shaken vigorously in plastic bags containing distilled water to dislodge attached diatoms (Taylor et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect epilithic diatom samples from hard substrates and ropes.

steps.description: All diatoms samples were preserved in 4% formalin before transferring to the laboratory for cleaning the diatoms frustules.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve biological samples using formalin solution.

steps.description: Motile epipelic diatoms were trapped by lens tissue that left on the mud surface for one day.
steps.searchable_summary: Trap motile diatoms using lens tissue on sediment surface.

steps.description: Lens tissue were removed and cut into small pieces and put in screw glass tubes, followed by shaking vigorously in distilled water (Al-Shaheen & Abdullah, 2021).
steps.searchable_summary: Process lens tissue traps to dislodge diatoms.

steps.description: The supernatant of epipelic and epilithic diatom were transferred into clean pyrex beakers containing H2O2 (35%), heated to clean the diatoms frustules.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate supernatant containing diatoms from sediment/debris.

steps.description: The supernatant of epipelic and epilithic diatom were transferred into clean pyrex beakers containing H2O2 (35%), heated to clean the diatoms frustules.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean diatom frustules using hydrogen peroxide and heat.

steps.description: Finally, they washed with distilled water then mounted by Naphrax® to prepare permanent slide (Al- Handal & Wulff, 2008).
steps.searchable_summary: Wash cleaned diatom frustules with distilled water.

steps.description: Finally, they washed with distilled water then mounted by Naphrax® to prepare permanent slide (Al- Handal & Wulff, 2008).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare permanent microscope slides of diatom frustules using mounting medium.

steps.description: Microscopic examination of diatoms was done using Zeiss Axiophot 2 imaging microscope (Carl Zeiss AB) that is supplied with a professional camera for sample imaging.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform light microscopy examination and imaging of diatom slides.

steps.description: SEM imaging of diatoms was done using Hitachi S-3500N SEM (Hitachi, Tokyo, Japan) operated at 15 kV at the Knight Oceanographic Research Center, College of Marine Science, University of South Florida, Florida, USA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform scanning electron microscopy (SEM) imaging of diatoms.

steps.description: All measurements of diatom species are in micrometres (μm).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure dimensions of diatom species from images.
